outcome_text: Policies and procedures are in place that address AI risks associated with third-party entities, including risks of infringement of a third-party's intellectual property or other rights.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI supply chain risk management, including assessing third-party AI component risks, evaluating intellectual property implications of third-party AI software and data, and establishing contractual and procedural safeguards for third-party AI relationships.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI supply chain risk management, including assessing third-party AI component risks, evaluating intellectual property implications of third-party AI software and data, and establishing contractual and procedural safeguards for third-party AI relationships.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of vendor and third-party risk management as it applies to data platform providers, AI model suppliers, cloud infrastructure vendors, and data service providers.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-6.1 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-014 is the strongest match (STS: 0.694, cosine: 0.738). Secondary: LS-K-011 (LS, cosine: 0.723), AG-T-002 (AG, cosine: 0.649).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.694). Shared vocabulary: [ai, data, knowledge, management, party, risk, third]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assessing, chain, component, contractual, establishing, evaluating] vs WIDAI terms [applies, cloud, infrastructure, model, platform, providers] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.901 (P=0.901, R=0.900). Balanced P/R — the 33-word competency implication and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.738 ≈ STS 0.694 (gap 0.044). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, data, knowledge, management, party] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.1%. Shared tokens: [ai, data, knowledge, management, party, risk, third]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assessing, chain, component, contractual, establishing, evaluating]. WIDAI-only: [applies, cloud, infrastructure, model, platform, providers].
